title: Empty repo summer planning app
repo_state: Empty Git repository with no README, app files, or context folder.
prompt: Use $arch. I want to build a summer app.

expected_first_question.question: What product are we building, and who is the first target user?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: A summer planning web app for one friend-group organizer who needs to collect trip ideas, availability, and final decisions in one place.

options.label: Second option
options.text: A family summer activity planner for parents who need to coordinate events, budgets, and schedules.

options.label: Other
options.text: The developer describes a different product and first target user.

expected_first_question.why_recommended: It narrows a vague seasonal app into one user, one job, and a web workflow that can be validated quickly.

confirmed_answer_flow.decision: Build a friend-group summer planning web app for one organizer.
Value proof is creating one plan with invitees, availability, ideas, and a selected decision.

red_flags: asks all seven intake questions at once
adds payments or analytics dashboard to v1

title: AI founder spec generator
repo_state: Fresh repository with product name only.
prompt: Use $arch. Build a product where founders turn messy startup ideas into specs AI coding agents can implement.

expected_first_question.question: Who is the first target user, and what messy idea-to-spec job must v1 solve?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: Solo technical founders who need to turn one rough product idea into an implementation-ready context folder and feature specs.

options.label: Second option
options.text: Agencies that need to turn client discovery notes into scoped build plans.

options.label: Other
options.text: The developer names another first buyer/user and job.

expected_first_question.why_recommended: Solo founders make the smallest first user segment and map directly to ARCH-style context output.

confirmed_answer_flow.decision: Start as a local-first CLI if the developer wants to prove workflow before web auth.

red_flags: builds a marketplace or team workspace in v1
stores unvalidated model output as trusted state

title: Existing Next.js SaaS rescue
repo_state: Next.js app with package.json, app routes, Prisma schema, and no context folder.
prompt: Use $arch to inspect this repo and create context so Codex can finish the MVP without drifting.

expected_first_question.question: What is the one MVP workflow this existing app must complete first?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: Preserve the existing Next.js and Prisma stack, then define one end-to-end authenticated user workflow as the MVP.

options.label: Second option
options.text: Pause feature work and first document architecture, data ownership, and incomplete routes before selecting the workflow.

options.label: Other
options.text: The developer identifies a different first workflow or constraint.

expected_first_question.why_recommended: Existing code should be preserved unless it blocks the MVP; one workflow gives future agents a clear path.

confirmed_answer_flow.decision: Preserve the current stack and define the first complete authenticated workflow.

red_flags: ignores existing package and schema files
fails to bootstrap context before continuing

title: Developer CLI for repo cleanup
prompt: Use $arch. I want a tool that cleans up messy repos before agents work on them.

expected_first_question.question: What is the first cleanup job and who runs it?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: A local CLI for individual developers that scans a repo and writes a cleanup report plus safe suggested file changes.

options.label: Second option
options.text: A GitHub Action that comments on pull requests with cleanup recommendations.

options.label: Other
options.text: The developer describes another workflow or runner.

expected_first_question.why_recommended: A CLI avoids auth and hosting while proving whether the cleanup workflow is useful.

title: Mobile habit accountability app
repo_state: No code yet. User says mobile matters.
prompt: Use $arch. I want to build a habit accountability app with friends.

expected_first_question.question: Who is the first user and what accountability loop must v1 prove?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: A responsive web app for one small friend group to create one shared habit, check in daily, and see streak status.

options.label: Second option
options.text: An Expo mobile app if push notifications and native mobile use are required on day one.

options.label: Other
options.text: The developer specifies a different first user or mobile requirement.

expected_first_question.why_recommended: Responsive web proves the behavior faster; Expo is justified if native notifications are core to the value proof.

confirmed_answer_flow.decision: Use Expo because push notifications are required for v1.

red_flags: builds complex social feeds in v1

title: Internal ops dashboard
repo_state: Existing spreadsheet process, no app code.
prompt: Use $arch. We need an internal dashboard for support escalations.

expected_first_question.question: What repeated support decision must the dashboard make easier first?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: A single-role internal web app where support leads triage open escalations by owner, severity, and next action.

options.label: Second option
options.text: Keep the spreadsheet and add an import/report generator before building a full dashboard.

options.label: Other
options.text: The developer names a different first operational decision.

expected_first_question.why_recommended: It focuses on the repeated decision instead of building a broad analytics product.

confirmed_answer_flow.decision: Build a single-role escalation triage web app.

red_flags: adds complex permissions before one role works

title: High-stakes health advice product
repo_state: Empty repo with vague healthcare idea.
prompt: Use $arch. I want an AI app that gives people medical advice from symptoms.

expected_first_question.question: What safe health-adjacent job can v1 support without diagnosing or replacing a clinician?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: A symptom note organizer that helps users prepare structured notes and questions for a licensed clinician.

options.label: Second option
options.text: A clinic-facing intake summarizer used by authorized staff with clinician review.

options.label: Other
options.text: The developer specifies a safer regulated workflow and review boundary.

expected_first_question.why_recommended: Direct medical advice is high risk; a note organizer can deliver value while preserving clinician judgment.

confirmed_answer_flow.decision: Build a clinician-prep note organizer, not a diagnosis tool.

red_flags: omits clinician review or disclaimer boundary
